text: In 2011, a team led by Britta Hölzel at Massachusetts General Hospital scanned participants before and after an eight-week Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) course. Post-program MRI images revealed increased gray matter density in the left hippocampus, the posterior cingulate cortex, and the temporo-parietal junction — regions involved in learning, self-referential processing, and perspective-taking. Notably, the amygdala shrank, and participants reported lower perceived stress. Eight weeks of structured attention practice had altered brain architecture in measurable ways. The cortex does not simply reflect a life of attention; it is sculpted by it, continuously and without exception.
question: Which brain region shrank following the eight-week MBSR program in Hölzel's study?

options.text: The hippocampus, which showed decreased gray matter density
The temporo-parietal junction, associated with perspective-taking
The posterior cingulate cortex, involved in self-referential processing
The amygdala, associated with stress reactivity
